title: Course: COSC 474 - Artificial Intelligence in Cybersecurity
description: Introduces undergraduate students to foundational machine learning concepts and their applications in cybersecurity. Students learn how AI enhances threat detection, automates responses, and identifies cyber risks. Topics include supervised and unsupervised learning, deep learning, explainable AI (xAI), adversarial machine learning, and ethical concerns in security AI. Hands-on labs and case studies reinforce technical and ethical understanding.
prerequisites_text: COSC 220 and MATH 312 completed with grades of C or higher
content:
COSC 474 - Artificial Intelligence in Cybersecurity
Credits: 3
Prerequisites: COSC 220 and MATH 312 completed with grades of C or higher
Offered: Fall, Spring

Description: Introduces undergraduate students to foundational machine learning concepts and their applications in cybersecurity. Students learn how AI enhances threat detection, automates responses, and identifies cyber risks. Topics include supervised and unsupervised learning, deep learning, explainable AI (xAI), adversarial machine learning, and ethical concerns in security AI. Hands-on labs and case studies reinforce technical and ethical understanding.
